title: Recursion, Recurrences, and Divide-and-Conquer · Correctness Defense
summary: Break a large document and spatial search problem into independently checkable subproblems. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a recursive solver with recurrence evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define base cases, progress measures, and combination rules before recursive calls.
Trace call trees, stack depth, repeated subproblems, and returned values.
Write and interpret recurrences for divide, solve, and combine costs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.instruction: Order the Recursive Contract correctness-and-cost proof for this quiz task before implementing it.
